channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: Taiwan Incident Highlights Cybersecurity Gaps in Rail Systems
item.description: A Taiwanese student experimenting with software-defined radio technology shut down three bullet trains for nearly an hour, leading to an anti-terrorism response.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: SecurityScorecard Snags Driftnet to Level Up Threat Intelligence
item.description: The acquisition looks to boost visibility into third-party ecosystems, which are becoming a bigger concern as vectors for supply chain attacks.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Maximum Severity Cisco SD-WAN Bug Exploited in the Wild
item.description: This is the second time this year a threat actor has leveraged a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Cisco's network control system.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
item.description: Cisco has released updates to address a maximum-severity authentication bypass flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller that it said has been exploited in limited attacks. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, carries a CVSS score of 10.0. "A vulnerability in the peering authentication in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly SD-WAN vSmart, and Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Stealer Backdoor Found in 3 Node-IPC Versions Targeting Developer Secrets
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about what has been described as "malicious activity" in newly published versions of node-ipc. According to Socket and StepSecurity, three different versions of the npm package have been confirmed as malicious -
node-ipc@9.1.6 node-ipc@9.2.3 node-ipc@12.0.1
"Early analysis indicates that node-ipc@9.1.6, node-ipc@9.2.3, and node-ipc@12.0.1
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
item.description: Attackers uniquely fingerprint victims before delivering spear-phishing payloads aimed at espionage, in the latest campaign from the Belarussian nation-state threat group.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: PAN-OS RCE, Mythos cURL Bug, AI Tokenizer Attacks, and 10+ Stories
item.description: Everything is still on fire. This week feels dumb in the worst way — bad links, weak checks, fake help desks, shady forum posts, and people turning supply chain attacks into some cursed little game for clout and cash. Half of it feels new. Half of it feels like crap we should have fixed years ago. The mess keeps getting louder: users get tricked, boxes get popped, tools meant for normal work
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Upcoming Speaking Engagements
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Mustang Panda Linked to Updated FDMTP Backdoor in Asia-Pacific Espionage Campaign
item.description: Mustang Panda campaign deploys updated FDMTP backdoor against Asia-Pacific and Japan networks

item.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukrainian Government With Geofenced PDF Phishing, Cobalt Strike
item.description: The Belarus-aligned threat group known as Ghostwriter has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting governmental organizations in Ukraine. Active since at least 2016, Ghostwriter has been linked to both cyber espionage and influence operations targeting neighboring countries, particularly Ukraine. It's also tracked under the monikers FrostyNeighbor, PUSHCHA, Storm-0257, TA445, UAC‑0057
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Enhancing Data Center Security Without Sacrificing Performance
item.description: <p>For AI data centers, where the stakes are the highest and performance constraints are the tightest, security and performance are no longer a zero-sum game.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/enhancing-data-center-security-without-sacrificing-performance/">Enhancing Data Center Security Without Sacrificing Performance</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Nadir Izrael

item.title: New Linux Kernel Vulnerability Fragnesia Allows Root Privilege Escalation
item.description: <p>The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46300, is similar to the recently disclosed exploits named Dirty Frag and Copy Fail.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/new-linux-kernel-vulnerability-fragnesia-allows-root-privilege-escalation/">New Linux Kernel Vulnerability Fragnesia Allows Root Privilege Escalation</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Google Launches Android Spyware Forensics Tool for High-Risk Users
item.description: Google’s Android Advanced Protection Mode is getting a new feature allowing trusted security experts to investigate potential spyware infections

item.title: AI Drives Cybersecurity Investments, Widening 'Valley of Death'
item.description: In a role reversal, investment dollars in security startups exceeded the value of mergers and acquisitions in 1Q26 by more than $1 billion, a rare occurrence.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: New Fragnesia Flaw Hands Linux Local Users Root Access
item.description: New Fragnesia kernel flaw lets unprivileged local users escalate to root on Linux systems

item.title: Mythos Proves Potent in Vulnerability Discovery, Less Convincing Elsewhere
item.description: <p>Independent benchmarking finds Mythos highly effective for source code audits, reverse engineering, and native-code analysis, though its exploit validation and reasoning capabilities remain inconsistent.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/mythos-proves-potent-in-vulnerability-discovery-less-convincing-elsewhere/">Mythos Proves Potent in Vulnerability Discovery, Less Convincing Elsewhere</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend

item.title: Akamai to Acquire AI and Browser Security Firm LayerX for $205 Million
item.description: <p>The acquisition enables Akamai to expand its Zero Trust portfolio to add protection directly into the browser.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/akamai-to-acquire-ai-and-browser-security-firm-layerx-for-205-million/">Akamai to Acquire AI and Browser Security Firm LayerX for $205 Million</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Chinese APTs Expand Targets, Update Backdoors in Recent Campaigns
item.description: <p>Salt Typhoon has hit an energy entity in Azerbaijan. Twill Typhoon has targeted Asian entities with an updated RAT.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/chinese-apts-expand-targets-update-backdoors-in-recent-campaigns/">Chinese APTs Expand Targets, Update Backdoors in Recent Campaigns</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Foxconn Attack Highlights Manufacturing's Cyber Crisis
item.description: A Nitrogen ransomware attack on Foxconn's North American facilities is one of 600 hits on manufacturers this year, as gangs increasingly target the sector for its low tolerance for downtime.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: PraisonAI CVE-2026-44338 Auth Bypass Targeted Within Hours of Disclosure
item.description: Threat actors have been observed attempting to exploit a recently disclosed security vulnerability in PraisonAI, an open-source multi-agent orchestration framework, within four hours of public disclosure. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-44338 (CVSS score: 7.3), a case of missing authentication that exposes sensitive endpoints to anyone, potentially allowing an attacker to invoke the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: How AI Hallucinations Are Creating Real Security Risks
item.description: AI hallucinations are introducing serious security risks into critical infrastructure decision-making by exploiting human trust through highly confident yet incorrect outputs. When an AI model lacks certainty, it doesn’t have a mechanism to recognize that. Instead, it generates the most probable response based on patterns in its training data, even if that response is inaccurate. These outputs
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: G7 Countries Release AI SBOM Guidance
item.description: <p>The goal of the guidance, which outlines minimum elements, is to help organizations enhance transparency in AI systems and supply chains. </p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/g7-countries-release-ai-sbom-guidance/">G7 Countries Release AI SBOM Guidance</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: How Dangerous Is Anthropic’s Mythos AI?
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: F5 Patches Over 50 Vulnerabilities
item.description: <p>The company’s latest quarterly advisory describes high and medium-severity issues in BIG-IP, BIG-IQ, and NGINX.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/f5-patches-over-50-vulnerabilities/">F5 Patches Over 50 Vulnerabilities</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Hackers Targeted PraisonAI Vulnerability Hours After Disclosure
item.description: <p>The first exploitation attempts were observed less than four hours after the authentication bypass was publicly disclosed.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/hackers-targeted-praisonai-vulnerability-hours-after-disclosure/">Hackers Targeted PraisonAI Vulnerability Hours After Disclosure</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: Windows Zero-Days Expose BitLocker Bypasses And CTFMON Privilege Escalation
item.description: An anonymous cybersecurity researcher who disclosed three Microsoft Defender vulnerabilities has returned with two more zero-days involving a BitLocker bypass and a privilege escalation impacting Windows Collaborative Translation Framework (CTFMON). The security defects have been codenamed YellowKey and GreenPlasma, respectively, by the researcher, who goes by the online aliases Chaotic Eclipse
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Most Organizations Now Use AI Agents for Sensitive Security Tasks
item.description: Semperis study finds 74% of organizations believe AI will increase attacks on identity infrastructure

item.title: ICO Publishes Five-Step Plan to Counter Emerging AI-Powered Attacks
item.description: The Information Commissioner’s Office has released new guidance on how to mitigate the risk of AI-powered attacks

item.title: High-Severity Vulnerability Patched in VMware Fusion
item.description: <p>The patch was announced as Broadcom is attending the Pwn2Own hacking competition in Berlin this week.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/high-severity-vulnerability-patched-in-vmware-fusion/">High-Severity Vulnerability Patched in VMware Fusion</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs

item.title: Researcher Drops YellowKey, GreenPlasma Windows Zero-Days
item.description: <p>YellowKey is a BitLocker bypass that requires physical access. GreenPlasma enables elevation of privileges to System.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.securityweek.com/researcher-drops-yellowkey-greenplasma-windows-zero-days/">Researcher Drops YellowKey, GreenPlasma Windows Zero-Days</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.securityweek.com">SecurityWeek</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire

item.title: New Fragnesia Linux Kernel LPE Grants Root Access via Page Cache Corruption
item.description: Details have emerged about a new variant of the recent Dirty Frag Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability that allows local attackers to gain root access, making it the third such bug to be identified in the kernel within a span of two weeks. Codenamed Fragnesia, the security vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2026-46300 (CVSS score: 7.8) and is rooted in the Linux kernel's XFRM
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 18-Year-Old NGINX Rewrite Module Flaw Enables Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open, including a critical flaw that remained undetected for 18 years. The vulnerability, discovered by depthfirst, is a heap buffer overflow issue impacting ngx_http_rewrite_module (CVE-2026-42945, CVSS v4 score: 9.2) that could allow an attacker to achieve remote code execution or cause a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Checkbox Assessments Aren't Fit to Measure Risk
item.description: Security governance needs to be more than an annual compliance exercise. New companies are emerging to address risk-management gaps in current audit tools.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: Attackers Weaponize RubyGems for Data Dead Drops
item.description: Threat actors are publishing RubyGems packages that include scrapers targeting public-facing UK government servers, but with no clear objective.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Tables Turn on 'The Gentlemen' RaaS Gang With Data Leak
item.description: An OPSEC failure provides a window into what helped the ransomware group rise: a generous affiliate model, opportunistic TTPs, and an effective organizational structure.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Six MCP Security Incidents Every Security Leader Should Know | UpGuard
item.description: From registry poisoning to filesystem wipes: discover the 6 MCP security incidents every leader must know to secure their AI agent workflows in 2026.

item.title: AI GitHub Agents: How One Issue Leaked Private Repos | UpGuard
item.description: Uncover how indirect prompt injection and tool poisoning allow attackers to exfiltrate private data via AI agents—all without stealing a single credential.

item.title: Dark Reading Celebrates 20 Years as a Leading Authority on Cybersecurity, Highlighting the People, Events, Ideas, and Technologies Shaping the Modern Risk Landscape
item.description: Informa TechTarget's flagship cybersecurity media brand launches a special content series to mark two decades as a trusted source for cybersecurity professionals.

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday Update Targets 120 Security Flaws
item.description: <p>Microsoft’s May Patch Tuesday fixes 120 flaws, including 31 remote code execution bugs, with no zero-days reported at release.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-microsoft-may-patch-tuesday-120-flaws/">Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday Update Targets 120 Security Flaws</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Google Launches New Android Security Features to Fight Scams, Theft
item.description: <p>Google detailed Android security updates for 2026, including verified bank calls, stronger theft protection, OTP hiding, and spyware forensics.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-android-security-scam-calls-theft-spyware/">Google Launches New Android Security Features to Fight Scams, Theft</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Canvas Owner Reaches Agreement With Cybercriminals After Ransomware Attack
item.description: Instructure says it reached an agreement with ShinyHunters over the Canvas breach data

item.title: Hackers Claim 11M Files Stolen From Foxconn, Supplier to Apple and Nvidia
item.description: <p>Foxconn confirmed a North American cyberattack after Nitrogen claimed it had stolen 11M files tied to major tech customer projects.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-foxconn-ransomware-11m-files-apac/">Hackers Claim 11M Files Stolen From Foxconn, Supplier to Apple and Nvidia</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: Avada Builder Flaws Expose One Million WordPress Sites
item.description: Avada Builder flaws allowed file read and SQL injection on one million WordPress sites

item.title: Canvas Breach Hackers Reach Deal After Claiming 275M Records Stolen
item.description: <p>Instructure reached a deal with the Canvas hackers after they claimed to have stolen data tied to nearly 9,000 schools and 275 million people.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-canvas-breach-hackers-deal-275m-records-stolen/">Canvas Breach Hackers Reach Deal After Claiming 275M Records Stolen</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: Microsoft's MDASH AI System Finds 16 Windows Flaws Fixed in Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled a new multi-model artificial intelligence (AI)-driven system called MDASH to facilitate vulnerability discovery and remediation at scale, adding that it's being tested by some customers as part of a limited private preview. MDASH, short for multi-model agentic scanning harness, is designed as a model-agnostic system that uses bespoke AI agents for different vulnerability
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Azerbaijani Energy Firm Hit by Repeated Microsoft Exchange Exploitation
item.description: A threat actor with affiliations to China has been linked to a "multi-wave intrusion" targeting an unnamed Azerbaijani oil and gas company between late December 2025 and late February 2026, marking an expansion of its targeting. The activity has been attributed by Bitdefender with moderate-to-high confidence to a hacking group known as FamousSparrow (aka UAT-9244), which shares some level of
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: LatAm Vibe Hackers Generate Custom Hacking Tools on the Fly
item.description: In the latest evolution of automated cyberattacks, threat actors heavily leveraged AI agents to support campaigns against entities in Mexico and Brazil.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: China's 'FamousSparrow' APT Nests in South Caucasus Energy Firm
item.description: The cyberthreat group targets an Azerbaijani oil and gas firm with repeated attacks, as the China-linked actors extend targeting beyond hospitality, telecom, and government sectors.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Ransomware: Over Half of CISOs Would Consider Paying Ransom to Hackers
item.description: Survey of cybersecurity leaders suggests that majority would strongly consider paying cybercriminals, if that’s what it took to help restore encrypted systems

item.title: [Webinar] How Modern Attack Paths Cross Code, Pipelines, and Cloud
item.description: TL;DR: Stop chasing thousands of "toast" alerts. Join experts from Wiz to learn how hackers connect tiny flaws to build a "Lethal Chain" to your data—and how to break it. Register for the Strategic Briefing Here. Most security tools work like a smoke alarm that goes off every time you burn a piece of toast. You get so many alerts that you eventually start to ignore them. The real danger? While
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Most Remediation Programs Never Confirm the Fix Actually Worked
item.description: Security teams have never had better visibility into their environments and never been worse at confirming what they fix stays fixed. Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report puts the mean time to exploit at an estimated negative seven days. The Verizon 2025 DBIR puts median time to remediate edge device vulnerabilities at 32 days. These numbers have understandably driven the industry toward a clear
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 is as Good as Mythos at Finding Security Vulnerabilities
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Global Cyber Agencies Issue New SBOMs for AI Guidance to Tackle AI Supply Chain Risks
item.description: The G7 Cybersecurity Working Group releases new SBOM for AI guidance, outlining seven key data clusters to boost transparency and security across AI supply chains

item.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities spanning its product portfolio, although none of them have been listed as publicly known or under active attack. Of the 138 flaws, 30 are rated Critical, 104 are rated Important, three are rated Moderate, and one is rated Low in severity. As many as 61 vulnerabilities are classified as privilege escalation bugs, followed by
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: UK Cybersecurity Market Expands to £14.7bn with Strong Growth in AI Security Firms
item.description: UK cybersecurity sector reaches £14.7bn in revenue, driven by rapid growth in AI security firms, increased investment and rising employment across the industry

item.title: Microsoft Fixes 17 Critical Flaws in May Patch Tuesday
item.description: Microsoft has patched 120 vulnerabilities in this month’s security update round

item.title: GemStuffer Abuses 150+ RubyGems to Exfiltrate Scraped U.K. Council Portal Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to a new campaign dubbed GemStuffer that has targeted the RubyGems repository with more than 150 gems that use the registry as a data exfiltration channel rather than for malware distribution. "The packages do not appear designed for mass developer compromise," Socket said. "Many have little or no download activity, and the payloads are repetitive,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Android Adds Intrusion Logging for Sophisticated Spyware Forensics
item.description: Google on Tuesday unveiled a new opt-in Android feature called Intrusion Logging for storing forensic logs to better analyze sophisticated spyware attacks. Intrusion Logging, available as part of Advanced Protection Mode, enables "persistent and privacy-preserving forensics logging to allow for investigation of devices in the event of a suspected compromise," the company said. The feature, it
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Proofpoint Launches Dedicated MSP Business Unit and Introduces 365 Total Protection for North America
Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
item.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: It's Patch Tuesday for Microsoft &amp; Not a Zero-Day In Sight
item.description: It's the first time in two years with no zero-days. But with 137 flaws to patch, including nine critical ones, admins still have plenty of work to do.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Over 1 Million Baby Monitors, Security Cameras Exposed Through Meari Flaws
item.description: <p>Meari IoT flaws reportedly exposed baby monitor images, camera activity, and device data across more than 1 million connected devices.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-meari-iot-baby-monitor-camera-vulnerabilities/">Over 1 Million Baby Monitors, Security Cameras Exposed Through Meari Flaws</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: New Exim BDAT Vulnerability Exposes GnuTLS Builds to Potential Code Execution
item.description: Exim has released security updates to address a severe security issue affecting certain configurations that could enable memory corruption and potential code execution. Exim is an open-source Mail Transfer Agent (MTA) designed for Unix-like systems to receive, route, and deliver email. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-45185 (CVSS score: 9.8), aka Dead.Letter, has been described as a
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Launches 'Daybreak' to Help Build Secure By Design Software
item.description: With Daybreak, OpenAI wants its frontier AI models to be used to deploy secure by design software from the ground up

item.title: 1 in 15 MCP Servers are Lookalikes: Is Your Org at Risk? | UpGuard
item.description: For every official MCP server, up to 15 lookalikes exist. Learn to identify these registry-layer threats and discover methods to protect your organization.

item.title: MCP: The AI Protocol Quietly Expanding Your Attack Surface | UpGuard
item.description: As AI tools adopt MCP, security teams face a new shadow IT crisis. Learn what MCP is, why these servers carry risk, and how to begin gaining visibility.

item.title: RubyGems Suspends New Signups After Hundreds of Malicious Packages Are Uploaded
item.description: RubyGems, the standard package manager for the Ruby programming language, has temporarily paused account sign ups following what has been described as a "major malicious attack." "We're dealing with a major malicious attack on RubyGems right now," Maciej Mensfeld, senior product manager for software supply chain security at Mend.io, said in a post on X. "Signups are paused for the time being.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Hits TanStack npm Packages
item.description: Mini Shai-Hulud compromises TanStack npm packages and spreads across PyPI

item.title: Hugging Face Packages Weaponized With a Single File Tweak
item.description: A tokenizer library file present in Hugging Face AI models can be manipulated to hijack the model's outputs and exfiltrate data.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: End‑to‑End Encrypted RCS Messaging Arrives Across iPhone and Android
item.description: Apple begins rolling out end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging between iPhone and Android in iOS 26.5

item.title: Google Says Hackers Used AI to Build Zero-Day Exploit
item.description: <p>Google says hackers used AI to help build a zero-day exploit targeting 2FA, raising concerns about AI-assisted hacking.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-hackers-ai-zero-day-exploit/">Google Says Hackers Used AI to Build Zero-Day Exploit</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: New TrickMo Variant Uses TON C2 and SOCKS5 to Create Android Network Pivots
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new version of the TrickMo Android banking trojan that uses The Open Network (TON) for command-and-control (C2). The new variant, observed by ThreatFabric between January and February 2026, has been observed actively targeting banking and cryptocurrency wallet users in France, Italy, and Austria. "TrickMo relies on a runtime-loaded APK&nbsp; (dex.module),
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 20 Leaders Who Built the CISO Era: 2 Decades of Change
item.description: As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary special coverage, we profile the CISOs, founders, researchers, criminals, and policymakers who rewrote the enterprise risk playbook.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Editorial Team

item.title: Attackers Combine ClickFix With PySoxy Proxying to Maintain Persistence
item.description: Exploitation of open-source tools allows attackers to maintain persistent access after initial social engineering, warn ReliaQuest researchers

item.title: Webinar: What the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered - and How Radiant Security Can Help
item.description: Why do the Riskiest SOC Alerts Go Unanswered? Security operations teams are drowning in alerts. But the real problem isn't always alert volume; it's the blind spots. The most dangerous alerts are the ones no one is investigating. A recent report from The Hacker News examined why certain high-risk alert categories - WAF, DLP, OT/IoT, dark web intelligence, and supply chain signals- consistently
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Compromises TanStack, Mistral AI, Guardrails AI & More Packages
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recentsupply chain attack spree, has been linked to the compromise of the npm and PyPI packages from TanStack, UiPath, Mistral AI, OpenSearch, and Guardrails AI as part of a fresh Mini Shai-Hulud campaign. The affected npm packages have been modified to include an obfuscated JavaScript file ("router_init.js") that's designed to profile the execution
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Worm Redux: Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Infections Bite Supply Chain
item.description: Hundreds of npm packages infected by the self-propagating, credential-stealing worm from TeamPCP are related to the open source TanStack ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Copy.Fail Linux Vulnerability
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Why Agentic AI Is Security's Next Blind Spot
item.description: Agentic AI is already running in production environments across many organizations today. It is executing tasks, consuming data, and taking actions — most likely without meaningful involvement from the security team. The industry conversation has largely framed this as a question of policy: allow it, restrict it, or monitor it? However, that framing misses the point.&nbsp; The more urgent
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Malicious Hugging Face Repository Typosquats OpenAI
item.description: HiddenLayer reveals infostealer malware in a Hugging Face repository

item.title: South Staffordshire Water Fined £1m After Data Breach
item.description: The ICO has fined South Staffordshire Water nearly £1m for a series of data protection failings

item.title: Instructure Reaches Ransom Agreement with ShinyHunters to Stop 3.65TB Canvas Leak
item.description: American educational technology company Instructure, the parent company of Canvas, said it reached an "agreement" with a decentralized cybercrime extortion group after it breached its network and threatened to leak stolen information from thousands of schools and universities. In an update shared on Monday, the Utah-based firm said it "reached an agreement with the unauthorized actor involved in
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: OpenAI Launches Daybreak for AI-Powered Vulnerability Detection and Patch Validation
item.description: OpenAI has launched Daybreak, a new cybersecurity initiative that brings together frontier artificial intelligence (AI) model capabilities and Codex Security to help organizations identify and patch vulnerabilities before attackers find a way in using the same issues. "Daybreak combines the intelligence of OpenAI models, the extensibility of Codex as an agentic harness, and our partners across
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: iOS 26.5 Brings Default End-to-End Encrypted RCS Messaging Between iPhone and Android
item.description: Apple on Monday officially released iOS 26.5 with support for end-to-end encryption (E2EE) to Rich Communication Services (RCS) in beta as part of a "cross-industry effort" to replace traditional SMS with a more secure alternative. To that end, E2EE RCS messaging is rolling out to iPhone users running iOS 26.5 with supported carriers and Android users on the latest version of Google Messages.
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: FCC Softens Ban on Foreign-Made Routers
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission eased some restrictions and pushed back deadlines for foreign router manufacturers, but the ban is still in place.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Tech Can't Stop These Threats — Your People Can
item.description: Security controls can do only so much. Here are four attacks where your employees are usually your first, and only, line of cyber defense.
item.dc:creator: A. Stryker

item.title: FCC Robocall Crackdown Raises Privacy Concerns Over Mandatory ID Checks
item.description: <p>The FCC’s proposed robocall crackdown could force carriers to verify customer identities, raising privacy concerns over anonymous phone use.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-fcc-robocall-crackdown-privacy-id-checks/">FCC Robocall Crackdown Raises Privacy Concerns Over Mandatory ID Checks</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: TeamPCP Compromises Checkmarx Jenkins AST Plugin Weeks After KICS Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Checkmarx has confirmed that a modified version of the Jenkins AST plugin was published to the Jenkins Marketplace. "If you are using Checkmarx Jenkins AST plugin, you need to ensure that you are using the version 2.0.13-829.vc72453fa_1c16 that was published on December 17, 2025 or previously," the cybersecurity company said in a statement over the weekend. As of writing, Checkmarx has released
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mac Users Warned Over Fake Claude Install Instructions
item.description: <p>Hackers are using Google Ads and Claude shared chats to target Mac users with fake setup instructions that can install malware.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-claude-mac-download-google-ads-malware/">Mac Users Warned Over Fake Claude Install Instructions</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: cPanel CVE-2026-41940 Under Active Exploitation to Deploy Filemanager Backdoor
item.description: A threat actor named Mr_Rot13 has been attributed to the exploitation of a recently disclosed critical cPanel flaw to deploy a backdoor codenamed Filemanager on compromised environments. The attack exploits CVE-2026-41940, a vulnerability impacting cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM) that could result in an authentication bypass and allow remote attackers to gain elevated control of the control
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 1.8 Billion Gmail Users May Want to Check This AI Privacy Setting
item.description: <p>Google’s new Gmail AI personalization features are raising privacy concerns. Here’s what users should know and how to review smart settings.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-gmail-ai-personalization-privacy-settings/">1.8 Billion Gmail Users May Want to Check This AI Privacy Setting</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Esther Shein

item.title: Spotlight On: Worldline, a New Principal Participating Organization
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: Hackers Used AI to Develop First Known Zero-Day 2FA Bypass for Mass Exploitation
item.description: Google on Monday disclosed that it identified an unknown threat actor using a zero-day exploit that it said was likely developed with an artificial intelligence (AI) system, marking the first time the technology has been put to use in the wild in a malicious context for vulnerability discovery and exploit generation. The activity is said to be the work of cybercrime threat actors who appear to
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: TrickMo Variant Routes Android Trojan Traffic Through TON
item.description: ThreatFabric finds new TrickMo Android banking trojan variant routing C2 through The Open Network

item.title: 'Dirty Frag' Exploit Poised to Blow Up on Enterprise Linux Distros
item.description: The privilege escalation vulnerability, which is similar to other Linux flaws like Copy Fail and Dirty Pipe, may already be under limited exploitation.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Rushed Patches Follow Broken Embargo on New Linux Kernel Vulnerabilities
item.description: Two new high-severity vulnerabilities, dubbed ’Dirty Frag’ when chained, have been found in the Linux kernel, affecting most Linux distributions

item.title: Fake Claude Code Page Pushes PowerShell Stealer at Devs
item.description: Ontinue uncovers fake Claude Code installer pushing PowerShell stealer abusing Chrome's IElevator2

item.title: Hackers Use AI for Exploit Development, Attack Automation
item.description: Cyber adversaries have long used AI, but now attackers are using large language models to develop exploits and orchestrate complex attacks.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Hackers Observed Using AI to Develop Zero-Day for the First Time
item.description: Google Threat Intelligence Group details how cybercriminals attempted to launch a campaign based around an AI-developed Zero-Day targeting open-source software

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Rootkit, macOS Crypto Stealer, WebSocket Skimmers and More
item.description: Rough Monday. Somebody poisoned a trusted download again, somebody else turned cloud servers into public housing, and a few crews are still getting into boxes with bugs that should’ve died years ago — the same old holes, same lazy access paths, same “how the hell is this still open” feeling. One report this week basically reads like a guy tripped over root access by accident and decided to stay
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
